mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions compress wage growth expectations and signal peak cyclical demand, triggering a short-term equity pullback as valuation multiples contract over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint divergence between manufacturing output and residential construction activity signals a structural shift in capital allocation; when INDPRO rises while HOUST falls, equity markets price in a productivity-driven earnings expansion that lifts S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward returns show no directional asymmetry relative to the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain normalization, rising industrial production directly translates to higher corporate margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of INDPRO uptrend instances, or the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real personal consumption expenditures and retail sales data reflects resilient aggregate demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 1-year windows, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative GDPC1 growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to compress equity risk premiums and delay capital expenditure cycles, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand shock; this joint signal reduces recession probabilities and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term's marginal effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward returns show no directional asymmetry relative to the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market downturn, falling housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, which mechanically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk aversion spikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of HOUST decline instances, or the rolling 3-month correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns fails to cross -0.20.
